name: Pace
description_short: Pace University is a private institution offering undergraduate, graduate, and online degree programs across four campuses in New York, with focus on experiential learning and career preparation.
description_long:
Pace University is a private educational institution headquartered in New York with campuses in Manhattan, Westchester County, and White Plains. Founded as a comprehensive university, Pace serves approximately 9,000+ students across undergraduate, graduate, and online programs. The institution positions itself as a career-focused university leveraging New York's business and cultural ecosystem.

Pace offers undergraduate degrees in business, computer science, game development, arts and entertainment management, nursing, medicine preparation, and cybersecurity. Graduate programs include MBA, law (through Haub Law School), and specialized master's degrees. The university emphasizes experiential education through internships, co-ops, practicums, and clinicals—students completed 9,000+ such experiences last year. Online options are available for students unable to attend physical campuses.

The university distinguishes itself through its New York location providing access to Fortune 500 companies, Broadway theaters, Silicon Alley startups, and major healthcare institutions. Rankings include Top 6% nationally for return on investment (Georgetown CEW), Top 8% for business majors (PayScale), and nationally recognized environmental law program. Athletics compete at NCAA Division II level on the Westchester campus.

Pace is best suited for students seeking structured career preparation with hands-on experience rather than those prioritizing research output or Ivy League prestige. The institution serves traditional undergraduates, adult learners, online students, transfers, international students, and veterans. Location-dependent experience quality—NYC campus offers urban professional networks while Westchester provides traditional campus life.
services: Undergraduate degree programs across multiple disciplines
Juris Doctor and law degree programs (Haub Law School)
Online degree programs for distance learners
Internship and co-op placement programs (9,000+ annually)
NCAA Division II athletics and sports programs
Research opportunities through partnerships with global organizations
Four physical campus locations (NYC, Westchester, White Plains Law, Online)
Student support services and veteran-specific programs
pros: 9,000+ internships, co-ops, practicums, field experiences, and clinicals completed by students annually
Top 6% nationally for return on investment according to Georgetown Center on Education and the Workforce
Four distinct campus experiences (NYC, Westchester, Law School, Online) providing flexibility
Nationally recognized environmental law program at Haub Law School
Direct access to internship and research opportunities at Fortune 500 companies and major NYC institutions
NCAA Division II athletics with modern facilities on Westchester campus
Robust online education program for non-traditional and distance learners
cons: Website contains no transparent information about tuition costs or financial aid availability
No mention of graduation rates, student debt statistics, or employment outcomes data
Limited information about merit scholarships or need-based financial aid programs
Smaller institution—not ranked in top national university rankings (US News, etc.)
No disclosed student-to-faculty ratio or class size information on homepage
best_for: Students seeking career-focused education with guaranteed internship and co-op placements
NYC-based or commuting students wanting to leverage New York's business and cultural opportunities
Working adults and non-traditional students who need online or evening program flexibility
Students prioritizing hands-on experiential learning over pure research or prestige rankings
diagnosis: Pace University is a career-focused private institution best for students seeking structured experiential education and internship placement within the New York metro area. CRITICAL CAVEAT: This is an educational institution, NOT a consumer finance company—it was miscategorized in CreditDoc's database and should be removed or recategorized to 'education' as it offers no financial products, loans, credit services, or consumer finance solutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
